transcript: Hello everybody. My name is Swen. I'm co-founder at Light Protocol. And you might have heard about us last year. We're not super in the spotlight usually, but last year at Breakpoint we launched a protocol called zkCompression. We launched it together with Helius Labs. And what it does is, just to give you a brief context, it reduces the data storage costs on Solana by orders of magnitude. And so it's a very powerful primitive, but I'm gonna talk about some very specific products that we've built on top of this this year. And that's what I'm gonna focus this talk about on. So, and a little hint, it's about fixing rent, specifically for Solana DeFi and payments. Now, you might wonder, first question, okay, so why does Solana need to fix rent for DeFi and internet finance more broadly? Well, Solana wants to be the market for everything, right? The everything exchange. And so, meaning anything that wants to be tokenized should be tradable on Solana. Now, to that end, we all know Solana has a very clear goal, which is 1 million TPS capacity. And they call it increase bandwidth, reduce latency. Now, the world also has billions of people. And so if you want to bring a billion plus people on chain, and their ideas and their wallets, then what do you do? Well, you need to fix rent. Specifically, right now on Solana, if you want to coin something, this could be a piece of content, a piece of data, you actually pay five dollars roughly for that market and for that coin. So most of that cost is going into various different accounts. Those are token accounts, PDA accounts, the mint account that you use when you... that you get when you use the SPL token program, and metadata accounts. And structurally, these markets and coins, they're kind of permanent. You can't really close them again. And so what that means is this five dollar cost is effectively a fee or a tax that you incur on Solana right now. Now, if we look at the competition, the competition right now is much cheaper. Actually roughly a hundred times cheaper. So if you coin something on Base for instance, it's roughly five cents. Now, I think we all agree, I think we should be much slower with the rent exemption cost on Solana. And so, you know, it would be nice if we could undercut Base even. Maybe something like two cents would be quite nice. And effectively what this is, is we need to IBRL, but also for account creation cost. And so that's why we've built over the last couple of months the Light Token Program, which we are announcing today. And it's basically a high performance token standard that is specifically for rent-free internet capital markets. And so what does that mean? It's kind of like SPL token. It feels like SPL token. But it's also 200 times cheaper to create these mint accounts and these token accounts that you use when you interact with the SPL token program. Now, there are three very important things that you need to deal with if you want to build a token standard that actually is usable in DeFi. And this is kind of from all our learnings from building zkCompression, bringing it to market, and learning from customer feedback etc., what worked, what didn't work. And so there's three main things. Well, first of all, it has to be rent-free, right? That's the value prop that you take if you want to compete against something like the SPL token standard. This is fixed. You know, 200 times cheaper allocation cost. We figured that out a while back. So actually 200 times cheaper, if you look at it, it's roughly in that area that we want to get to, right? So it's actually undercutting Base. We're cheaper on the Solana L1 than we are on the Base L2, which is kind of nice. And now the second thing, that's the most difficult part, is your program has to be super high performance, right? So it needs to be at least like SPL token with regards to performance. Performance meaning compute consumption as you do transfers, as you do trades on chain. Otherwise your new token standard will have no chance in the market. So basically we figured that out with our Light Token Program. Turns out it's actually pretty competitive with SPL token and then also with the upcoming P-token in specific hot paths. So, and the last part is you want to have great UX. And that's super important because there are a lot of different DeFi players and clients. So when we designed the Light Token Program and the SDKs, we really thought through that and how we can create really great UX and make it easy for people to upgrade. So we spent a lot of time on this to get the SDKs right. The solution to this is the Token Interface SDK. If you interacted with Token-22, which is another SPL token standard, and if you've built something that supports this token standard, you kind of already use token interfaces. And it's a really neat design. You basically say, well, there's an interface and it kind of supports all these different token standards. And so what we've built is an SDK that does just that. So here you have an example, just example Raydium fork that we made rent-free. And you can see it's just, you know, using the Anchor's existing InterfaceAccount feature, it kind of just works. This is just the program side on this side. So I'm gonna show you a couple of quick examples, walk you through the snippets really quick. You can see that the interface is matched, the call signatures are very similar. On the left side you see the SPL token program's SDK, and then that includes SPL token and Token-22 tokens. And then on the right side you see our transfer interface SDK which includes the Light Token as well. So it supports all three. Now I'm gonna play this demo real quick on Devnet. So the token program is on Devnet right now. And you can see it's just a lot of setup. On the left side you see we stream with Laserstream, which is listening to the transaction events. And we're seeing a token transfer and then there you see it's extremely efficient on the CU cost. And also what this transfer did is, in the first instruction it's a compute budget, in the second instruction it's creating an ATA account, an associated token account. And you see it's extremely cheap, it's much cheaper than the SPL ATA program. And then in the third instruction is a regular transfer from one Light Token account to another Light Token account. And interestingly you can also see that the actual fee paid for the token account is much lower than what you'd pay to allocate an SPL token account. Cool. Anyways, so to move on and go to kind of a quick summary to showcase all the different features of this kind of rent-free stack. We now have rent-free ATAs, already seen that. Rent-free SPL support, so the Light Token Program actually supports wrapping with SPL. And then we have program-owned token accounts as well, mint accounts, and also token metadata, and it's all rent-free. So here's just a quick example to show you that it's very easy using these macros to create a new mint with metadata. It's very simple. And then just very quickly I want to go into the how. I'll spare you of all the deep dives on the tech, but essentially the insight is that you can distinguish between hot state and cold state. And so what you see is you have a DeFi pool for instance, it's hot state, it's very active. But at some point, and you see this if you look at the chain, you look at the block explorers with memecoin launchpads etc., you will see that these accounts become dormant at some point. And so when that happens, our miners can compress them into Merkle trees. You see that there. And that's using zkCompression by the way. And then now the last part is really just, you might be wondering, well how do you load a compressed account without actually impacting the UX of all these different clients and programs? And we do that with this little helper, it's called the load instruction. And so it just dynamically checks if a transaction interacts with a cold account and it loads these accounts atomically into the transaction. And you can, in many cases you can actually do this atomically. For instance on Raydium you can actually load a whole cold market in one transaction and still execute a trade on it. Cool. So to close this up, it's live on Devnet right now, the token program. And coming to Mainnet really soon. And the SDKs are live, you can use them. If you scan the QR code you'll get to the Twitter account which gets you to the docs. It's all open source. And I hope you check it out and start building with it. You know, if you're a DeFi team, launchpad, or even a payments company looking to reduce the storage cost of ATAs, hit me up, find us, we're here at the conference. You can DM us on Twitter as well. All right, thank you.

sources.title: Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform  | Drift Updates
sources.snippet:
Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform  | Drift Updates
[![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6310e7dee49f0866da8eed4c/6867a1f64ca2c75f0d14f371_16c97ce9017b907297827c5113a068f9_Frame%201597880158.avif)](https://www.drift.trade/)
[
Launch App
](#)
## Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform
December 4, 2025
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6310e7dee49f08e5798eed50/693023e13e4b72ada17e0fe2_2025-12-03%2017.19.13.jpg)
[
Contents
](#)
[
Contents
](#)
[
](https://twitter.com/driftprotocol)[
](https://discord.com/invite/fMcZBH8ErM)
[
Trade Now
](https://app.drift.trade/)
![]()
![]()
Drift v3 is the protocol’s biggest performance upgrade yet. Rebuilt around trader execution, v3 delivers**10x faster fills, 10x improved liquidity, and a cleaner, more intuitive interface**than v2.
This upgrade was built for one thing: outperformance. Instant execution. Deeper liquidity. A suite of tools that helps you trade faster and with more confidence.
Drift v2 was the first to bring cross-margin and multi-asset collateral to DeFi on-chain trading. We built on Solana to deliver NASDAQ-level performance on-chain and with major Solana upgrades like Alpenglow, Firedancer and Jito’s Block Assembly Market (BAM), Drift is built to outperform as the network scales.
Since launch, Drift has surpassed 1.5B in TVL, $133B+ in total trading volume and $650M in open interest. Drift v3 is built to take those numbers and your trading even further.
**Drift v3 will be available on Dec 4, 2025.**New users will need an invite code to access Drift v3.
**DM**[**@DriftProtocol**](https://x.com/DriftProtocol)**to request access.**
## Why v3?
Drift v3 represents the protocol’s next stage of maturity: the point where years of infrastructure, liquidity, and execution improvements converge into a faster, clearer, more reliable trading experience.
**Over the past year, Drift has evolved in three critical areas:**
1. Execution speed
2. Liquidity depth
3. Trader experience## How Drift v3 outperforms
*The road to v3 was focused on improving liquidity, trading execution and speed. Here’s what that looks like in practice:*
### **Trading Execution and Speed**
* **10x faster order fills:**On Drift v3, 85% of market orders are filled within the same slot. A slot is Solana’s smallest time unit (about 400 milliseconds). On Drift, that means that trades are executed in under half a second. Instant execution.
* **15x faster take profit/stop loss triggers:**For BTC, it dropped from 15 slots (\~6 seconds) to 1 slot (\~0.4 seconds). This means your risk controls and exits now trigger immediately, reducing slippage and missed opportunities.
* **Gasless trading by default:**Unlike other DEXs, where you pay gas on every trade, Drift abstracts gas fees so that’s one less thing to worry about eating into profits
* **400ms oracle updates**: Prices update every 400 millisecond (roughly 2.5 times per second). This keeps quotes accurate and reduces the chance of stale pricing.
* **Scales with Solana:**As Solana increases block size and throughput, Drift automatically gets faster too, handling more trades per second.### **Liquidity**
* **10x reduction in slippage on market orders:**In Drift v2, large market orders could move the price by 20 bps. In v3, that’s been cut to just 2 bps. Traders now get far better pricing, especially on size.
* **5x tighter TP/SL execution:**Take-profit and stop-loss orders are far more precise. On major markets like SOL and BTC, fills are 4x tighter, while non-majors see up to 5x improvement. This means exits trigger closer to the price you set, reducing losses and protecting gains.
* **Introduction of Drift Liquidity Provider (DLP)**: Provides liquidity for both Perps and Spot markets. Community members can deposit funds into DLP to support market-making and earn a share of the pool's profits. Currently in the testing stage. Public launch in Q1 2026.
* **Expanded Market Maker Program:**With v3’s expanded market maker initiatives, long-tail altcoins now hav

tags.impact_reason: Light Protocol's Light Token Program aims to drastically reduce the cost of token creation on Solana, potentially improving its competitiveness and scalability for DeFi and broader applications.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a powerful compression chamber as the 'Semantic Anchor' because the story is about 'zkCompression' literally crushing data storage costs. The block being destroyed represents the financial barrier of 'state rent' on Solana, which the transcript highlights as a major problem. For the homepage, I integrated the diegetic text 'RENT' engraved onto this block. This visually frames 'rent' as the physical obstacle being obliterated by Light Protocol's technology, directly narrating the 'Rent-Free' headline.
visual_idea.description: In a vast, dark, industrial chamber, two colossal, smooth obsidian plates of a hydraulic press are closing vertically. Caught between them is a large, translucent amber-colored crystal block, glowing faintly from within. The word 'RENT' is deeply engraved on the face of the crystal block. The obsidian plates crackle with faint blue energy lines as they apply immense pressure, causing the crystal to develop a network of bright, internal fractures that illuminate the scene. The background recedes into deep, atmospheric darkness, with distant, out-of-focus architectural structures hinting at immense scale.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A colossal hydraulic press, forged from two vertically closing, smooth, polished obsidian plates, dominates a vast, dark, industrial chamber. Between these monumental plates, a large, translucent amber-colored crystal block is caught, faintly glowing from within. The word 'RENT' is deeply engraved into the face of this struggling crystal. Faint blue energy lines crackle across the obsidian plates as they exert immense pressure, causing a mesmerizing network of bright, internal fractures to propagate within the crystal, brilliantly illuminating the immediate scene. The background recedes into an abyss of deep, atmospheric darkness, with distant, out-of-focus brutalist sci-fi architectural structures subtly hinting at an immense, overwhelming scale. The composition emphasizes extreme verticality (3:4 aspect ratio), with ample negative space above, making the press feel truly monumental. Cinematic volumetric lighting permeates the scene, highlighting dramatic shadows and reflections on the polished obsidian and the glowing crystal. Hyperrealistic textures, intense dramatic contrast, and high dynamic range. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a powerful compression chamber as the 'Semantic Anchor' because the story is about 'zkCompression' literally crushing data storage costs. The block being destroyed represents the financial barrier of 'state rent' on Solana, which the transcript highlights as a major problem. For the homepage, I integrated the diegetic text 'RENT' engraved onto this block. This visually frames 'rent' as the physical obstacle being obliterated by Light Protocol's technology, directly narrating the 'Rent-Free' headline.
visual_idea.description: A macro, detailed shot focused on the point of impact. We see the mirror-smooth, dark surface of the obsidian press making contact with the crystal block. The letters of the engraved word 'RENT' are visible but are actively being distorted and shattered by the pressure. A brilliant, hot-white light erupts from a spiderweb of micro-fractures spreading through the crystal, creating intense highlights and lens flare. The focus is on the textures of pressure, energy, and material failure.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A macro, detailed, square-framed shot, centrally focused on the precise point of impact. The frame is filled with the dramatic interaction of materials. A mirror-smooth, dark, polished obsidian press, characteristic of Brutalist Sci-Fi aesthetics, makes forceful contact with a translucent crystal block. Deeply engraved letters forming the word 'RENT' are visibly embedded within the crystal, but they are actively being distorted, fractured, and shattered by the immense pressure. A brilliant, hot-white cinematic volumetric light erupts from a dense spiderweb of micro-fractures aggressively spreading through the crystal, creating intense highlights and dramatic lens flare that pierces through the darkness. The scene powerfully emphasizes the raw textures of pressure, kinetic energy, and catastrophic material failure, with high contrast and sharp details. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic, cinematic contrast, high dynamic range, volumetric lighting, 1:1 aspect ratio.

editorial.headline: Light Protocol Unveils "Rent-Free" Token Standard to Undercut Base
editorial.standfirst: Leveraging zkCompression, the new Light Token Program slashes asset creation costs by 200x, effectively eliminating Solana's state rent barrier for DeFi and payments.
key_takeaways: The Light Token Program reduces mint and account creation costs from ~$5.00 to ~$0.02, undercutting L2 competitors like Base.
The architecture utilizes a hybrid state model, keeping active assets "hot" for performance while compressing dormant accounts into Merkle trees via zkCompression.
Live on Devnet with a Mainnet launch imminent, the standard offers drop-in SDK compatibility for major protocols like Raydium and Anchor-based projects.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
In a bid to solve one of Solana's most persistent economic bottlenecks, **Light Protocol** has introduced the Light Token Program, a new asset standard designed to reduce on-chain state costs by orders of magnitude. While Solana creates a high-throughput environment for trading, the underlying cost of "rent"—roughly $5 to initialize the necessary Mint, Token, and Metadata accounts for a new asset—has functioned as a regressive tax on innovation. Co-founder Swen Schaeferjohann argues that for Solana to function as the "everything exchange," it must not only compete on bandwidth but also on the unit cost of state, targeting a price point of roughly two cents to undercut Layer-2 competitors like **Base**.

The technical unlock behind this efficiency is **zkCompression**, a primitive launched previously in partnership with **Helius**. The Light Token Program bifurcates state into "hot" and "cold" tiers. Active liquidity pools and high-frequency trading accounts remain uncompressed to ensure maximum performance, matching the compute unit (CU) efficiency of the legacy SPL Token program. Conversely, dormant state—such as inactive holder accounts or long-tail memecoin markets—is cryptographically compressed into Merkle trees. Crucially, the protocol introduces an atomic "load instruction," allowing these compressed accounts to be decompressed and interacted with in a single transaction, preserving composability for protocols like **Raydium**.

For institutional players and payment processors, the implications are significant. The current model imposes a linear cost scaling for every new user onboarded (via Associated Token Accounts or ATAs). By moving to a rent-free model, payment infrastructures can scale to millions of users without incurring prohibitive state bloat fees. This shift transforms account creation from a capital expenditure into a negligible marginal cost, aligning Solana's economic structure with the requirements of consumer-grade applications.

Adoption friction remains the primary adversary for new token standards. To mitigate this, **Light Protocol** has released a Token Interface SDK that mirrors existing Anchor patterns. This allows developers to support the new standard alongside Token-22 and legacy SPL tokens without rewriting core logic. With the program already live on Devnet and Mainnet deployment scheduled shortly, the protocol is positioning itself as the default infrastructure for the next generation of high-volume asset issuance.
